message:
fix(test-isolation): include PID + per-process seq in E2E run id and io_alias temp paths

Two related concurrency bugs surfaced by running the harness in
parallel cargo-test workers (and by the swarm running multiple
agents that each kick off cargo test simultaneously).

scripts/e2e_suite.sh + e2e_suite_test.rs (RUN_ID collisions)
  - RUN_ID was previously
        e2e-v${SUITE_VERSION}-$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)-s${E2E_SEED}
    so two e2e_suite.sh invocations launched in the same wall-clock
    second with the same seed (which the deterministic test harness
    deliberately does) raced on
    target/e2e_suite/${RUN_ID}/{trace.jsonl,artifact_index.json}.
  - Add `-p$$` (parent shell PID) into RUN_ID so concurrent runs at
    the same second + seed land in disjoint output directories.
  - Update e2e_suite_test.rs to introduce latest_e2e_seed_run_dir(),
    a seed-suffix-only matcher that finds the most recent
    e2e-...-s<seed> directory instead of relying on the prefix that
    no longer uniquely identifies a single run. The
    e2e_suite_runs_and_produces_jsonl test now pins seed = "43001"
    and uses the seed-keyed lookup, so it remains stable when a
    sibling test (or another worker) also wrote into target/e2e_suite.

io_abi_test.rs (`make_temp_file_fd` collisions)
  - The temp-file template was `franken_io_alias_${pid}_${stack-addr}`,
    but in test threads the stack address is reasonably stable across
    repeated invocations and PID is by definition shared inside the
    same test process. So nested make_temp_file_fd() calls in dup/dup2
    aliasing tests could land on the same path and stomp each other.
  - Add a static AtomicU64 TEMP_FILE_SEQ that is fetch_add(1)'d on
    every call and folded into the path as the new middle component
    `franken_io_alias_${pid}_${seq}_${stack-addr}`. The stack-addr
    component is preserved so two concurrent processes still
    differentiate without needing globally-unique seq values.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
